note: 20 complex exercises anchored on Julien's habitual work (ML/transformers, Python stdlib CLI/process, concurrency & protocols, Rust systems, front-end & data transforms). Objective checks: check=py runs the code against assertions; check=rust compiles with rustc --crate-type lib; check=judge is read by hand.

scenarios.rubric: Correct matmul, head split, scaling, causal mask, softmax, concat, output projection; numerically matches reference on identity-weight case.
Top-k restriction, softmax over survivors, cumulative nucleus with at-least-one guarantee, returns correct index set on known vector.
Contiguous repeat layout, correct output shape, no aliasing/mutation of input.
Correct half-split rotation, frequency schedule, pos=0 identity, matches reference values.

scenarios.prompt: Write a stdlib-only `class ProcessSupervisor` (no third-party deps) that manages one detached child process. Methods: `start(self, cmd, log_path)` launches `cmd` (list[str]) in its own session (start_new_session=True) with stdout+stderr redirected to `log_path`, and writes the child pid to a pid-file; `is_alive(self) -> bool` checks liveness via os.kill(pid, 0); `stop(self, timeout=5.0)` sends SIGTERM, waits up to `timeout` for exit, then SIGKILL if still alive, and removes the pid-file; `attach(cls, pid_file)` classmethod reconstructs a supervisor for an already-running pid. Be correct about the ESRCH/EPERM cases in is_alive and the race where the process exits during stop. Respond with ONE Python code block.
scenarios.rubric: Detached session, log redirect, robust is_alive (ESRCH vs EPERM), graceful SIGTERM->SIGKILL with timeout, pid-file persistence + attach, no zombie/leak.

scenarios.prompt:
Implement an incremental Server-Sent-Events (SSE) parser in pure Python. Define exactly:

    class SSEParser:
        def feed(self, chunk):  # chunk is bytes

`feed` accepts arbitrary byte chunks (event boundaries and even individual lines may be split across chunks) and returns a list of the events COMPLETED during this call, in order. Maintain an internal buffer across calls. An event ends at a blank line (\n\n). Each returned event is a dict {'event': <str or None>, 'data': <str>} where multiple `data:` lines are joined with '\n'. A leading space after the field colon is stripped. Lines beginning with ':' are comments and ignored. Decode bytes as utf-8. Respond with ONE Python code block.
scenarios.rubric: Cross-chunk buffering, blank-line event split, multi-line data join, comment skip, colon+space handling; correct on adversarially chunked input.

scenarios.prompt:
Implement crash-safe JSON state persistence in pure Python. Define exactly:

    def atomic_write_json(path, obj): ...
    def read_json(path): ...

`atomic_write_json` must serialize obj to JSON and write it ATOMICALLY: write to a temporary file in the SAME directory as `path`, flush and os.fsync it, close, then os.replace it onto `path` so a concurrent reader never observes a partial/truncated file. Clean up the temp file on error. `read_json` parses and returns the object. Use only stdlib. Respond with ONE Python code block.
scenarios.rubric: Temp-in-same-dir, fsync before replace, atomic os.replace, temp cleanup on error, correct roundtrip; no leftover temp files.

scenarios.prompt:
Build an argparse-based CLI parser in pure Python. Define exactly:

    def build_parser():   # returns argparse.ArgumentParser
    def parse(argv):      # returns (namespace, extras_list)

The parser has a subcommand `serve` with: a positional `name`; `--port` (int, default 8000); a repeatable `--serve-arg` that APPENDS each occurrence to a list (so argv ['serve','m','--serve-arg=--foo','--serve-arg','bar'] yields serve_arg == ['--foo','bar']); a `--allow-network` store_true flag. Any arguments after a literal `--` separator must be captured as `extras` and NOT parsed as options. `parse` runs parse_known_args (or REMAINDER) so that `extras` holds the post-`--` tokens. Respond with ONE Python code block.
scenarios.rubric: Subcommand, repeatable append (=VALUE and space forms), int port default, store_true, post---- forwarding into extras.

scenarios.prompt:
Implement an Anthropic->OpenAI request translator in pure Python. Define exactly:

    def anthropic_to_openai(req):  # req: dict, returns dict

Translate an Anthropic /v1/messages request into an OpenAI /v1/chat/completions request: (1) if req has a top-level string `system`, prepend a {'role':'system','content': <that string>} message; (2) for each message in req['messages'], content may be a plain string OR a list of blocks: text blocks ({'type':'text','text':..}) are concatenated into one string; tool_use/image/other blocks are ignored for content purposes; keep role; (3) map `max_tokens`->`max_tokens`, pass through `temperature` if present; (4) translate Anthropic `tools` (each {name, description, input_schema}) into OpenAI tools [{'type':'function','function':{'name','description','parameters': input_schema}}]; (5) always set 'model' from req.get('model','default'). Return the OpenAI dict. Respond with ONE Python code block.
scenarios.rubric: System hoisting, string|block content normalization, max_tokens/temperature mapping, tool schema translation, model passthrough.

scenarios.prompt:
Implement an OpenAI-stream -> Anthropic-SSE translator in pure Python. Define exactly:

    class StreamTranslator:
        def feed(self, chunk):  # chunk: an already-parsed OpenAI streaming chunk dict
        def done(self):         # returns any final events to flush

Both return a list of Anthropic event dicts {'type': ..., ...}. Emit, in order across the stream: exactly one 'message_start' before any text; then on the first text delta a 'content_block_start' {'type':'content_block_start','index':0,'content_block':{'type':'text','text':''}}; for each non-empty delta (chunk['choices'][0]['delta'].get('content')) a 'content_block_delta' {'type':'content_block_delta','index':0,'delta':{'type':'text_delta','text': <text>}}; when a chunk carries finish_reason, emit 'content_block_stop' {'index':0}, then 'message_delta' {'delta':{'stop_reason': <mapped>}} mapping 'stop'->'end_turn' and 'length'->'max_tokens', then 'message_stop'. `done()` flushes message_stop if the stream ended without a finish_reason. Never emit message_start or content_block_start twice. Respond with ONE Python code block.
scenarios.rubric: Single message_start, lazy content_block_start, per-token deltas, finish mapping (stop/length), correct terminal sequence, idempotent guards.
Bounded queue, exactly-once processing, input-order results, sentinel shutdown, join without deadlock.

scenarios.prompt:
Implement bounded async fan-out in pure Python (asyncio only). Define exactly:

    async def gather_bounded(coros, limit, timeout):

`coros` is a list of awaitables. Run at most `limit` concurrently (asyncio.Semaphore). Apply a per-task timeout of `timeout` seconds; a task that exceeds it is cancelled and its result is the string 'TIMEOUT'. Return results IN THE ORIGINAL ORDER of `coros`. Ensure no task is left pending when the function returns (cancel + await stragglers). Other exceptions raised by a coro should be returned as the exception object in its slot, not raised. Respond with ONE Python code block.
scenarios.rubric: Semaphore concurrency cap, per-task timeout->'TIMEOUT', original-order results, no pending tasks on return, exception capture.
Compiles; &self interior mutability; LRU eviction correct; O(1) structure; Send+Sync usable across threads.
Compiles; correct lifetime 'a on Item; advance-by-one overlap; None when exhausted; size==0 guard.
Compiles; Sender: Clone with refcount; Condvar-based blocking send(full)/recv(empty); recv returns None after all senders dropped; Drop impl updates count + notifies.
Identifies return-ref-to-local (owned) bug; fix returns the borrow into `text` (best) so it compiles; last block compiles.

scenarios.prompt: Produce ONE self-contained HTML file (inline <style> and vanilla <script>, no external resources, no frameworks/CDNs) that renders a sortable, filterable data table. Requirements: at least 8 data rows with columns Name, Status, Latency (ms), Updated; a live search box that filters rows by name as the user types; clickable column headers that toggle ascending/descending sort and are NUMERIC-AWARE for the Latency column; a dark/light theme toggle button; Status rendered as a colored pill (e.g. green/amber/red). Clean, modern styling. Output only the complete HTML in ONE code block.
scenarios.rubric: Valid single-file HTML; working live filter; numeric-aware sortable headers; theme toggle; status pills; no external deps; not truncated.

scenarios.prompt: Produce ONE self-contained HTML file (inline <style> only, NO JavaScript, no frameworks) with three responsive pricing cards: Starter, Pro, Enterprise. The Pro card is visually highlighted as 'Most popular'. Requirements: responsive layout that stacks on narrow viewports via flex/grid + a media query; a palette defined with CSS custom properties (:root variables); visible :focus-visible states on the CTA buttons for keyboard accessibility; sensible spacing/typography. Output only the complete HTML in ONE code block.
scenarios.rubric: Valid single-file HTML, no JS; 3 cards with Pro highlighted; responsive media query; CSS variables; :focus-visible accessibility; not truncated.
Header handled, ints summed per (region,product), blank lines skipped, correct nested dict on known input.
Hand-rolled LCS; valid diff (reconstruction invariant holds for both a and b); correct prefixes; no difflib.
